zh.Kettleburgh, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可凱特萊包
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Charles II於1305年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可時，他的父親Edward Charles I35歲，母親Alice of Suffolk30歲。他與Dyonyse of Suffolk結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1375年9月3日在他的家鄉享年70歲。

zh-hans.Kettleburgh, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可凱特萊包
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Charles II于1305年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可时，他的父亲Edward Charles I35岁，母亲Alice of Suffolk30岁。他与Dyonyse of Suffolk结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1375年9月3日在他的家乡享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Antoine, Andre, Francois, Patrice, Germaine, Magalie, Philippe, Yves, Alain, Jacques, Cecile.

French, Welsh, English, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Tanzania): from the French form of the ancient Germanic personal name Karl ‘man, husband, freeman’, which was Latinized as Carolus. In France the personal name was popular from an early date, due to the fame of the Emperor Charlemagne (c. 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus, i.e. Charles the Great). The Old French form Charles was briefly introduced to England by the Normans, but was rare during the main period of surname formation. It was introduced more successfully to Scotland in the 16th century by the Stuarts, who had strong ties with France, and was brought by them to England in the 17th century. Its frequency as a Welsh surname is attributable to the late date of Welsh surname formation. Old English Ceorl ‘peasant’ is also found as a byname, but the resulting Middle English form, Charl, with a patronymic in -s, if it existed at all, would have been absorbed by the French form introduced by the Normans. English variants pronounced with initial k- for the most part reflect the cognate Old Norse personal name Karl, Karli. Compare Carl .

English: in some cases, possibly a habitational name from Charles (Devon), or a post-medieval variant of Cherrill, with excrescent -s, perhaps by false association with the personal name Charles. Cherrill is either a habitational name from Cherhill in Wilshire, or a status name from Middle English cherl, cheril, charl, chirl ‘churl, bonded tenant, serf; peasant’.

variants.name: Carlino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carlo, Mario, Antonio, Dino, Elio, Francesco, Giusto, Pasquale, Rocco, Carmelo, Ciro, Liborio, Salvadore, Carmella, Emilio, Ernesto, Isidoro.

Italian: from a pet form of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles . Carlino was also a term denoting a medieval Italian coin, and it is possible that in some cases the surname arose as a nickname, possibly for a worthless fellow, since it was a coin of low value, or as an occupational name for a moneyer.

variants.name: Karl
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Lothar, Erwin, Erhard, Georg, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Ernst, Gerhard, Hartmut, Heinz.

German: from the German personal name Karl, from Old High German karl ‘man, husband, freeman’. The popularity of this name and its cognates in central and northern Europe was greatly enhanced by its status as a royal and imperial name; in particular it was bestowed in honor of the Frankish emperor Charlemagne (about 742–814; Latin name Carolus Magnus). This surname is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Croatia, but is relatively rare there. See also Carl and Charles .

variants.name: Carleo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Luigi, Armando, Gino, Nino, Rocco, Xavier.
Italian:
from the medieval Greek personal name Kaloleonor Kaloleos meaning ‘Leon the good’.

variants.name: Carles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Bienvenido, Blanca, Carlito, Cristina, Elva, Ernesto, Humberto, Juan Carlos, Mariana, Mariano.  French Emile, Alain, Marcel.
Catalan: from the personal name Carles, Catalan form of Charles .
French: regional variant of   Charles  ; compare 1 above.

variants.name: Carlisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Annamaria, Antonio, Salvatore.
Italian: from a diminutive, Carlino, of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Julio, Luis, Orlando, Ricardo, Aida, Alicia, Andres, Angel.  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Silvio, Antonio, Carmela, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlozzi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Carlo, Emidio, Enrico, Giuseppe.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Carlozzo, a derivative of Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carlos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Raul, Alicia, Jesus, Luis, Armando, Jaime, Carlos, Ruben.  Portuguese Fernandes, Joao, Joaquim.
Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Carlos, an equivalent of Charles .

variants.name: Carolus
meanings: Dutch (mainly Groningen) and German: from the personal name Carolus, Latinized form of ancient Germanic name Karl (see Charles ), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of the surname Karl or Carl . This surname is also established in South Africa.

variants.name: Carlone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Umberto, Angelo, Antonio, Biaggio, Corrado, Cosmo, Filippo, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Nichola, Pasquale.
Italian: from an augmentative of the personal name Carlo, an equivalent of Charles .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Charles II was born in 1305, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edward Charles I, was 35 and his mother, Alice of Suffolk, was 30. He married Dyonyse of Suffolk. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 3 September 1375, in his hometown, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Charles II est né en 1305, à Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward Charles I, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Alice of Suffolk, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Dyonyse of Suffolk. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 3 septembre 1375, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Charles II 1305 in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Charles I 35 und seine Mutter Alice of Suffolk 30. Er hat Dyonyse of Suffolk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 3. September 1375 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Charles II è nato il nel 1305 nel Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Edward Charles I, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Alice of Suffolk, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Dyonyse of Suffolk. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 3 settembre 1375, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.Kettleburgh, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクケットレブルグ
ja.Suffolk, England: イギリスイギリススフォルク
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Charles IIが1305年にイギリスイギリススフォルクで生まれた時，父Edward Charles Iは35歳，母Alice of Suffolkは30歳であった。Dyonyse of Suffolkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1375年9月3日に故郷で70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Charles II(이)가 1305년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Charles I(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice of Suffolk(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Dyonyse of Suffolk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1375년 9월 3일 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Charles II nasceu em 1305, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edward Charles I, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Alice of Suffolk, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Dyonyse of Suffolk. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 3 de setembro de 1375, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Charles II родился в 1305 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edward Charles I было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice of Suffolk, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Dyonyse of Suffolk. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 3 сентября 1375 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Charles II nació en 1305, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward Charles I, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Alice of Suffolk, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Dyonyse of Suffolk. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 3 de septiembre de 1375, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años.
